purpose: Pre-specified statistical comparison protocol for deterministic and hosted/local LLM submissions.

primary_endpoint.definition: Mean bounded 0-100 task score over the 50-task suite.
primary_endpoint.rationale: Normalizes heterogeneous startup situations while preserving task-specific business objectives.

secondary_endpoints.use: Interpretable success-rate companion to average score.

secondary_endpoints.definition: Report average score and solve rate separately for FND-001..FND-030 and FND-031..FND-050.
secondary_endpoints.use: Detect split imbalance and possible overfitting to development tasks.

secondary_endpoints.definition: Report solved/5 and average score for each of the 10 task families.
secondary_endpoints.use: Identify capability profile rather than only aggregate score.

secondary_endpoints.definition: Provider errors, invalid actions, over-budget decisions, latency, token use, and estimated cost.
secondary_endpoints.use: Distinguish business-decision failure from interface or API failure.

single_run_comparison.interval: Nonparametric bootstrap 95% CI over paired task gaps.
single_run_comparison.hypothesis_test: Two-sided random sign-flip permutation test over paired task gaps.
single_run_comparison.effect_size: Cohen dz over paired task gaps.
single_run_comparison.win_loss_tie: Count tasks where model_a score is greater than, less than, or equal to model_b.

repeated_sampling_comparison.when_required: Required for stochastic decoding studies, self-consistency ablations, reflection/multi-agent variants, or when reporting sampling variance.
repeated_sampling_comparison.aggregation: Report per-run average score and solve rate, then bootstrap across submitted runs for repeated-sampling intervals.
repeated_sampling_comparison.paired_repeated_option: When multiple models use identical repeat indices, compare matched repeat means with paired intervals.

multiple_comparisons.default_family: All pairwise model comparisons reported in the main leaderboard.
multiple_comparisons.adjustment: Holm-Bonferroni adjustment over primary endpoint p-values.
multiple_comparisons.reporting: Report both raw and adjusted p-values; do not hide non-significant comparisons.

required_reporting_fields: task count and missing task ids if any
redacted audit traces for representative success/failure cases
claim_rules: Do not claim one model is better unless the comparison covers all 50 tasks or the claim is explicitly scoped.
Do not compare raw money, final cash, or revenue as primary outcomes across tasks; use them as diagnostics.
Treat provider errors, invalid JSON, and invalid actions as benchmark outcomes, not discarded trials.
For hidden-holdout claims, report only evaluator-approved aggregate fields.
